title: roettlera rufipes
side_effects: Roettlera rufipes can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems due to the high concentration of essential oils in its leaves and flowers. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as interact with certain medications. Prolonged exposure to the plant's pollen can lead to anaphylaxis in some individuals, requiring immediate medical attention. Improper use of Roettlera rufipes can also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue in some people due to its toxic properties.
